proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher age_years is associated with longer pfs_months (positive Pearson correlation).
Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative association across the three ECOG strata 0/1/2).
stage_iv=1 patients have shorter mean pfs_months than stage_iv=0 patients.
has_brain_mets=1 patients have shorter mean pfs_months than has_brain_mets=0 patients.
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months (positive correlation).
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
Smoking_status differs in pfs_months across categories: current smokers have shorter pfs than former or never.
Squamous histology has shorter pfs_months than adenocarcinoma histology.
Female patients (sex_female=1) have shorter mean pfs_months than males.

analyses.code: stats.pearsonr(df['age_years'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r=+0.757, p<1e-300. Strong positive correlation.

analyses.code: f_oneway across ECOG 0/1/2
ttest_ind by stage_iv
analyses.result_summary: Stage IV mean=2.93 vs non-Stage-IV=4.36; diff=-1.44 months, p<1e-300.

analyses.code: ttest_ind by has_brain_mets
analyses.result_summary: Brain mets mean=2.74 vs no=3.66; diff=-0.92 months, p<1e-300.

analyses.code: stats.pearsonr(df['albumin_g_dl'], df['pfs_months'])
stats.pearsonr(df['weight_loss_pct_6mo'], df['pfs_months'])
f_oneway across smoking_status
analyses.result_summary: Means: current=2.99, former=3.61, never=3.67; F=433, p=3.7e-187.

analyses.code: ttest_ind by histology
analyses.result_summary: Squamous=2.81 vs adenocarcinoma=3.67; diff=-0.86 months, F=1523, p<1e-300.

analyses.code: ttest_ind by sex_female
analyses.result_summary: Female=3.32 vs male=3.52; diff=-0.20 months, p=7.7e-25.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_pembrolizumab=1 is associated with longer mean pfs_months than treatment_pembrolizumab=0 (overall).
treatment_sotorasib=1 is associated with longer mean pfs_months than treatment_sotorasib=0 (overall).
treatment_olaparib=1 is associated with longer mean pfs_months than treatment_olaparib=0 (overall).
treatment_osimertinib=1 is associated with longer mean pfs_months than treatment_osimertinib=0 (overall).

Treatment assignment is correlated with the matching biomarker (e.g., osimertinib more likely in EGFR+; sotorasib more likely in KRAS G12C+; olaparib more likely in BRCA2+; pembrolizumab more likely in TMB-high).

analyses.code: ttest_ind by treatment_pembrolizumab
analyses.result_summary: Pembro=3.41 vs none=3.45; diff=-0.035, p=0.08 (no significant overall effect).

analyses.code: ttest_ind by treatment_sotorasib
analyses.result_summary: Sotorasib=3.64 vs none=3.32; diff=+0.32, p=5.6e-49.

analyses.code: ttest_ind by treatment_olaparib
analyses.result_summary: Olap=3.41 vs none=3.44; diff=-0.025, p=0.26 (no significant overall effect).

analyses.code: ttest_ind by treatment_osimertinib
analyses.result_summary: Osim=3.43 vs none=3.43; diff=+0.004, p=0.87 (no overall effect).

analyses.code: Compute P(treatment | biomarker=1) and P(treatment | biomarker=0)
analyses.result_summary: Treatment prevalences are essentially identical regardless of biomarker status: e.g., osim P(EGFR+)=0.30 vs P(EGFR-)=0.30; sotorasib P(KRAS+)=0.35 vs P(KRAS-)=0.35; olap P(BRCA2+)=0.28 vs P(BRCA2-)=0.30; pembro P(TMB-high)=0.50 vs P(TMB-low)=0.50. Treatment assignment looks randomized with respect to biomarker status.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within KRAS G12C+ patients, treatment_sotorasib increases mean pfs_months relative to no sotorasib (positive effect).
Within KRAS G12C-negative patients, treatment_sotorasib has no effect on pfs_months.
Within EGFR-mutated patients, treatment_osimertinib increases mean pfs_months relative to no osimertinib.
Within BRCA2-mutated patients, treatment_olaparib increases mean pfs_months relative to no olaparib.
Pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months increases with PD-L1 TPS quartile (i.e., effect largest in Q4).
Within TMB-high patients, pembrolizumab increases mean pfs_months relative to no pembrolizumab.

analyses.code: ttest_ind sotorasib vs not within kras_g12c==1
analyses.result_summary: In KRAS G12C+ (n=6368): sotorasib=5.77 (n=2246) vs no=3.21 (n=4122); diff=+2.55 months, p=1.7e-202. Strongly positive.

analyses.code: ttest_ind sotorasib vs not within kras_g12c==0
analyses.result_summary: In KRAS G12C- (n=43632): sotorasib=3.33 vs no=3.33; diff=-0.005, p=0.80. No effect outside KRAS G12C+.

analyses.code: ttest_ind osimertinib vs not within egfr_mutation==1
analyses.result_summary: In EGFR+ (n=6517): osimertinib=3.59 (n=1973) vs no=3.60 (n=4544); diff=-0.014, p=0.81. No effect.

analyses.code: ttest_ind olaparib vs not within brca2_mutation==1
analyses.result_summary: In BRCA2+ (n=1519): olaparib=3.32 (n=417) vs no=3.31 (n=1102); diff=+0.010, p=0.93. No effect.

analyses.code: ttest_ind pembrolizumab vs not within PD-L1 quartile
analyses.result_summary: Pembro effect by PD-L1 quartile Q1..Q4: -0.003 (p=0.95), -0.027 (p=0.49), -0.038 (p=0.34), -0.072 (p=0.07). No positive trend; if anything weakly negative.

analyses.code: ttest_ind pembrolizumab vs not within tmb_high==1
analyses.result_summary: In TMB-high (n=14258): pembro=3.27 vs no=3.32; diff=-0.048, p=0.20. No effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment_sotorasib by KRAS G12C effect is modified by sex_female: among KRAS G12C+ patients, sotorasib increases pfs_months only in males (sex_female=0), with no effect in females.
In a multivariable linear model the three-way interaction treatment_sotorasib:kras_g12c:sex_female is negative (i.e., sex_female cancels the kras_g12c amplification of the sotorasib effect).

analyses.code: ttest_ind sotorasib vs not, stratified by sex within kras_g12c==1
analyses.result_summary: KRAS+ male (n=3508): sotorasib=7.88 vs no=3.23, diff=+4.64 months, p<1e-300. KRAS+ female (n=2860): sotorasib=3.18 vs no=3.19, diff=-0.012, p=0.88. Effect is concentrated in males.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ treatment_sotorasib*kras_g12c*sex_female + covariates
analyses.result_summary: Three-way interaction coefficient = -4.582, t=-213.6, p<1e-300; positive 2-way kras*soto coefficient ~+4.59 in males is fully cancelled by the 3-way term in females.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_olaparib has no clinically meaningful effect on pfs_months in any binary subgroup (sex, stage, brain mets, biomarker, ECOG, smoking, histology) — all subgroup mean differences are near zero with no significance after multiple-testing scrutiny.
Within BRCA2+ patients, olaparib has no effect even after stratifying by sex, stage, ECOG, or other biomarkers.

analyses.code: Stratified ttest_ind olaparib vs not across binary features
analyses.result_summary: All subgroup differences for olaparib are within ~|0.07| months and none reach p<0.05 except a borderline squamous histology cell (-0.078, p=0.05) that does not replicate in adjusted models. No subgroup shows a positive effect.

analyses.code: Stratified ttest_ind olaparib vs not within brca2==1, by each feature
analyses.result_summary: Within BRCA2+, olaparib effect by sex/stage/brain/EGFR/KRAS/ALK/STK11/TMB/ECOG ranges from -0.46 to +0.36 months but none reach p<0.05; the largest is a noisy +0.36 in stk11+ (p=0.27). No real signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_pembrolizumab has no clinically meaningful effect on pfs_months in any single-feature subgroup (PD-L1 high, TMB high, never-smokers, ECOG, histology, etc.).
Pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months is largest in the joint subgroup pdl1_tps>=0.5 AND tmb_high=1.

analyses.code: Stratified ttest_ind pembrolizumab vs not across features
analyses.result_summary: All subgroup differences fall in ~|0.13| months. The largest negative cell is stage_iv=0 (-0.10, p=0.002) and squamous histology (-0.07, p=0.04); none replicate to a meaningful positive effect anywhere.

analyses.code: Stratified means pembro vs not in (pdl1>=0.5) x (tmb_high) cells
analyses.result_summary: PD-L1 high & TMB high (n=3150): pembro=3.33 vs no=3.25, diff=+0.08, p=0.30. PD-L1 high & TMB low: -0.13 (p=0.01) (negative). PD-L1 low & TMB high: -0.085 (p=0.04). No supportive positive effect in the doubly-positive cell.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_osimertinib has no clinically meaningful effect on pfs_months in any feature subgroup including EGFR-mutated patients.
Within EGFR-mutated patients, osimertinib has no effect even after stratifying by sex, stage, brain mets, smoking, or histology.

analyses.code: Stratified ttest_ind osimertinib vs not across features
analyses.result_summary: All differences within ~|0.03| months in main features; the only nominally significant cells are alk_fusion=1 (+0.23, p=0.018) and brca2_mutation=1 (-0.24, p=0.04). Neither survives multiple comparisons.

analyses.code: Stratified ttest_ind osimertinib vs not within egfr==1, by each feature
analyses.result_summary: All sub-stratum differences within EGFR+ are within ~|0.36| months; none reach p<0.05. No replicable benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a fully adjusted multivariable linear model (all features and treatments), the only significant treatment main effect is treatment_sotorasib (positive); pembrolizumab, olaparib, and osimertinib are not associated with pfs_months. Major prognostic factors are age (positive), ECOG (negative), stage IV (negative), brain mets (negative), albumin (positive), weight loss (negative), smoking_status (current worse), histology (squamous worse), sex (female slightly worse), LDH (slightly negative), and brca2_mutation (slightly negative).
After full adjustment, age_years remains a strong positive predictor of pfs_months (~+0.17 months per year), unchanged from the unadjusted estimate.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ all features + all treatments
analyses.result_summary: R^2=0.89. Significant prognostic effects: age_years +0.168/yr (p<1e-300), ecog_ps -1.10 (p<1e-300), stage_iv -1.42 (p<1e-300), has_brain_mets -0.91 (p<1e-300), albumin +0.46 (p<1e-300), weight_loss -0.073 (p<1e-300), smoking former vs current +0.55, never vs current +0.55, histology squamous -0.72, sex_female -0.20, LDH -0.0004 (p=1.4e-28), brca2_mutation -0.11 (p=6.6e-9), kras_g12c +0.89 (driven by sotorasib effect). Treatment main effects: only sotorasib +0.32 (p<1e-300); pembro -0.006 (p=0.39), olap +0.002 (p=0.74), osim +0.001 (p=0.88).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across an exhaustive screen of every (treatment x feature) two-way interaction with the standard adjustment set, the strongest interactions all involve treatment_sotorasib (with kras_g12c and with sex_female); no other treatment shows any interaction reaching p<1e-3 with any feature.

analyses.code: Loop OLS pfs_months ~ base + trt*feature for each (trt,feature)
analyses.result_summary: Top interactions all sotorasib: sex_female -0.585 (p<1e-300), kras_g12c +2.534 (p<1e-300), smoking_status[never] -0.46 (p=4e-99), smoking_status[former] -0.20 (p=7e-38), alk_fusion -0.34 (p=2e-27), egfr_mutation -0.21 (p=7e-25), brca2_mutation -0.33 (p=2e-16), histology[squamous] +0.11 (p=1e-12), age_years +0.0035 (p=3e-7), tmb_high +0.076 (p=5e-7). Pembrolizumab top: nlr -0.009 (p=0.007); olap top: alkphos +0.0003 (p=0.03); osim top: pdl1 -0.073 (p=0.03). None reach the strength of the sotorasib interactions.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within KRAS G12C+ males, the sotorasib treatment effect is abolished by brca2_mutation=1 (i.e., sotorasib does not increase pfs_months in KRAS G12C+ male brca2_mutation+ patients).
Within KRAS G12C+ males, the sotorasib treatment effect is abolished by alk_fusion=1 (i.e., sotorasib does not increase pfs_months in KRAS G12C+ male alk_fusion+ patients).

The complete sotorasib responder subgroup is: kras_g12c=1 AND sex_female=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND alk_fusion=0; in this group sotorasib produces a large positive pfs_months effect (~+5 months), and no smaller subgroup within it improves the effect further.

analyses.code: ttest_ind sotorasib vs not within (kras_g12c=1 & sex_female=0 & brca2_mutation=1)
analyses.result_summary: KRAS+ male brca2+ (n=107): sotorasib=3.41 vs no=3.48, diff=-0.07, p=0.89. Effect abolished.

analyses.code: ttest_ind sotorasib vs not within (kras_g12c=1 & sex_female=0 & alk_fusion=1)
analyses.result_summary: KRAS+ male alk+ (n=138): sotorasib=2.35 vs no=2.64, diff=-0.29, p=0.50. Effect abolished.

analyses.code: ttest_ind sotorasib vs not within (kras_g12c=1 & sex_female=0 & brca2_mutation=0 & alk_fusion=0); also stratified by every other feature within that group
analyses.result_summary: Canonical responder subgroup (n=3266): sotorasib=8.23 (n=1154) vs no=3.25 (n=2112), diff=+4.99 months, p<1e-300. Within this group every further stratification (smoking, histology, ECOG, stage, brain mets, TMB, EGFR, STK11) yields a sotorasib effect ~+4.7 to +5.2; the effect is uniform.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a single multivariable model the 4-way interaction structure {sotorasib + kras_g12c + sex_female + brca2_mutation/alk_fusion} captures the entire treatment-effect heterogeneity for sotorasib: positive 2-way kras_g12c*soto coefficient, negative 3-way soto*kras_g12c*sex_female, negative 3-way soto*kras_g12c*brca2_mutation, and negative 3-way soto*kras_g12c*alk_fusion.

A T-learner gradient-boosting model trained separately on sotorasib-treated and untreated patients yields per-individual treatment effects whose mean is +4.16 in (kras_g12c=1 & sex_female=0 & brca2=0 & alk=0) and ~0 elsewhere; a regression tree fit on these ITE values splits first on kras_g12c then on sex_female, with consistent ~+4 effects in KRAS+ males and ~0 effects in all other leaves.

analyses.code: OLS with kras*soto*sex_female + soto:kras:brca2_mutation + soto:kras:alk_fusion + adjustment set
analyses.result_summary: Coefficients: soto:kras_g12c=+4.767 (p<1e-300), soto:kras_g12c:sex_female=-4.586 (p<1e-300), soto:kras_g12c:brca2_mutation=-2.740 (p<1e-300), soto:kras_g12c:alk_fusion=-2.409 (p<1e-300). All other treatments (pembro, olap, osim) main effects ~0 (p>0.1). R^2=0.975.

analyses.code: T-learner GradientBoostingRegressor + sklearn DecisionTreeRegressor on ITE
analyses.result_summary: Mean ITE: KRAS-=+0.02, KRAS+=+2.37, female=+0.03, male=+0.55, brca2-=+0.32, brca2+=+0.17, alk-=+0.33, alk+=+0.14. Canonical responder (KRAS+ male brca2- alk-): mean ITE=+4.16; everyone else: +0.05. Tree first splits on kras_g12c (>0.5) then sex_female; leaves in KRAS+ males have ITE 3.74-4.19, KRAS+ females 0.00-0.47.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive age-pfs_months association is real and not confounded by stage, ECOG, brain mets, or other features — adjusted age coefficient stays +0.17/yr with R^2 contribution >0.5.
Mean PFS rises monotonically across age quartiles (Q1 < Q2 < Q3 < Q4).

analyses.code: qcut age into 4 + adjusted OLS
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS by age quartile: Q1=1.33, Q2=2.78, Q3=3.97, Q4=5.69. Adjusted age_years coef +0.169/yr (p<1e-300). Effect persists when interacted with stage/ECOG/brain mets but is attenuated in those with worse baseline (age*stage_iv=-0.014, age*ecog_ps=-0.014, age*brain=-0.014).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Even allowing a triple interaction treatment_pembrolizumab*pdl1_tps*tmb_high in a fully adjusted model, no significant pembrolizumab benefit appears in any (PD-L1, TMB) cell.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ pembro*pdl1_tps*tmb_high + adjustment set
analyses.result_summary: Pembro main +0.007 (p=0.65), pembro:pdl1=-0.029 (p=0.47), pembro:tmb_high=-0.044 (p=0.12), pembro:pdl1:tmb_high=+0.098 (p=0.20). No significant term — pembrolizumab is null even in the subgroup it would be expected to help.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Even allowing triple interaction treatment_olaparib*brca2_mutation*sex_female in a fully adjusted model, no significant olaparib benefit appears in BRCA2+ patients of either sex.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ olaparib*brca2_mutation*sex_female + adjustment set
analyses.result_summary: olap=+0.012 (p=0.25), olap:brca2=-0.049 (p=0.44), olap:sex_female=-0.015 (p=0.35), olap:brca2:sex_female=+0.080 (p=0.39). No significant term — olaparib is null even in BRCA2+.

proposed_hypotheses.text: An adjusted model within EGFR+ patients (controlling for age, ECOG, stage, brain mets, smoking, histology, sex, albumin, weight_loss) shows no osimertinib benefit; and the osimertinib*egfr_mutation interaction in the full sample is also null.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS within EGFR==1 and 2-way interaction in full data
analyses.result_summary: Within EGFR+ (n=6517): adjusted osimertinib coef = -0.020 (p=0.22). Full sample interaction osim:egfr_mutation = -0.017 (p=0.47). No effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There are no statistically significant pairwise treatment-by-treatment interactions on pfs_months (i.e., combinations of pembro/sotorasib/olaparib/osimertinib do not synergize or antagonize beyond their main effects).

analyses.code: OLS with each pair treatment_X*treatment_Y in adjusted model
analyses.result_summary: All six pairwise interactions p>0.13 with coefficients within |0.012|. No combination effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative sex_female prognostic effect (~-0.20 months) is concentrated in KRAS G12C+ patients (where it is largely the consequence of sotorasib working only in males). In KRAS G12C-negative patients, sex has no prognostic effect on pfs_months.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS in KRAS- subset
analyses.result_summary: Full sample adjusted sex_female = -0.205 (p=3.3e-177). Within KRAS G12C- patients adjusted sex_female = +0.001 (p=0.86). The sex effect is essentially absent outside KRAS G12C+.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Relative to never-smokers, current smokers have shorter pfs_months and former smokers have slightly longer pfs_months (small positive); the smoking effect persists after full adjustment.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS with smoking_status reference=never
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted relative to never: current = -0.390 (p=1.4e-235), former = +0.093 (p=1e-18). Smoking effect is real and persistent.

proposed_hypotheses.text: PD-L1 TPS (pdl1_tps) has no prognostic association with pfs_months either as a linear or quadratic term, after adjustment for age, ECOG, stage, brain mets, sex, smoking, histology, albumin, and weight loss.
TMB-high (tmb_high) has no prognostic association with pfs_months after the same adjustment.

analyses.code: OLS adding pdl1_tps + I(pdl1_tps**2) to adjusted model
analyses.result_summary: pdl1_tps adj coef +0.015 (p=0.79); quadratic +0.004 (p=0.96). No prognostic value.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS coefficient for tmb_high in full model (iter 8)
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted tmb_high coef = -0.005 (p=0.47). No prognostic value.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Histology (squamous vs adenocarcinoma) modestly modifies the prognostic value of KRAS G12C: squamous patients have slightly higher PFS conditional on KRAS G12C than adenocarcinoma patients (positive squamous*kras_g12c interaction).

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ histology*kras_g12c + adjustment set
analyses.result_summary: histology[squamous]:kras_g12c = +0.024 (p=0.027). Tiny in magnitude (~+1 day). Marginal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is no subgroup in which treatment_sotorasib lowers pfs_months: in KRAS-negatives, KRAS+ females, KRAS+ male brca2+, and KRAS+ male alk_fusion+, the sotorasib effect is zero (not negative).

analyses.code: ttest_ind sotorasib vs not in each non-responder subgroup
analyses.result_summary: KRAS- (n=43632): diff=-0.005, p=0.80. KRAS+ female (n=2860): diff=-0.012, p=0.88. KRAS+ male brca2+ (n=107): diff=-0.07, p=0.89. KRAS+ male alk+ (n=138): diff=-0.29, p=0.50. All near zero.

proposed_hypotheses.text: An exhaustive two-feature subgroup screen for the non-sotorasib treatments (pembro, olap, osim) finds no subgroup with a clinically meaningful positive treatment effect (>0.3 months) and very strong p-value (<1e-5); the few nominally significant cells are small magnitude (|diff|<0.7) and not corroborated by adjusted models.

analyses.code: Loop over all (feature1=v1, feature2=v2) cells with n>=200 and report any |diff|>0.15 with p<0.05
analyses.result_summary: Top hits (sorted by p): pembro in (kras_g12c=1, stage_iv=0): -0.46 (p=1.5e-4) [negative]; osim in (brca2=1, tmb_high=0): -0.40 (p=4e-3) [negative]; osim in (brca2=1, stage_iv=1): -0.38 (p=4e-3) [negative]; osim in (alk_fusion=1, brca2=0): +0.25 (p=0.01); olap in (egfr=1, stk11=1): +0.33 (p=0.03). None survive multiple testing or replicate as adjusted positive effects. No targeted subgroup for pembro/olap/osim emerges.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The age benefit on pfs_months is smaller in patients with stage_iv=1, has_brain_mets=1, or higher ecog_ps (i.e., poor-prognosis features attenuate the per-year age benefit).

analyses.code: OLS with age_years*{stage_iv, has_brain_mets, ecog_ps}
analyses.result_summary: age:stage_iv = -0.014 (p<1e-79), age:has_brain_mets = -0.014 (p<1e-63), age:ecog_ps = -0.014 (p<1e-170). Effect of an extra year of age is attenuated by ~0.014 months per poor-prognosis feature, but remains positive (overall ~+0.19 in best-prognosis cells, ~+0.13-0.15 in worst).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The fully refined sotorasib responder definition is kras_g12c=1 AND sex_female=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND alk_fusion=0; the adjusted treatment effect rises monotonically as each restriction is added (overall +0.32 → KRAS+ +2.51 → KRAS+ male +4.57 → KRAS+ male brca2- +4.73 → KRAS+ male alk- +4.75 → KRAS+ male brca2- alk- +4.91).

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS within each cumulative subgroup with treatment_sotorasib + standard prognostic covariates
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted sotorasib coefficients: All=+0.323; KRAS+=+2.509; KRAS+ male=+4.574; KRAS+ male brca2-=+4.726; KRAS+ male alk-=+4.754; KRAS+ male brca2- alk-=+4.911 (all p<1e-300). Each restriction adds incremental adjusted effect, confirming the cumulative subgroup is the responder set.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for the full sotorasib*kras_g12c*sex_female*brca2*alk_fusion structure, the main effects of treatment_pembrolizumab, treatment_olaparib, and treatment_osimertinib remain near zero (|coef|<0.01) and non-significant (all p>0.1) in the full adjusted model.
Within sotorasib non-responders, no treatment among pembro/olap/osim shows any signal of effect (all |diff|<0.03, p>0.2).

Within the canonical sotorasib responder subgroup (KRAS+ male brca2- alk-), pembrolizumab is associated with a slightly shorter mean pfs_months (negative direction) — likely a chance/noise finding given that the same pembrolizumab effect is null in every other subgroup.

analyses.code: Final adjusted OLS with full sotorasib interaction structure + standard covariates + the three other treatments as main effects
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted: treatment_pembrolizumab = +0.001 (p=0.77), treatment_olaparib = +0.005 (p=0.12), treatment_osimertinib = +0.002 (p=0.48). Sotorasib structure: soto:kras=+4.77, soto:kras:sex_female=-4.59, soto:kras:brca2=-2.74, soto:kras:alk_fusion=-2.41 (all p<1e-300). Confirms three null treatments.

analyses.code: ttest_ind pembro/olap/osim vs not within the non-responder set
analyses.result_summary: Among non-responders (n=46734): pembro diff=-0.022 (p=0.25); olap diff=-0.027 (p=0.21); osim diff=+0.009 (p=0.66). All null.

analyses.code: ttest_ind pembrolizumab vs not within canonical sotorasib responder subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Within KRAS+ male brca2- alk- (n=3266): pembro=4.87 (n=1672) vs no=5.16 (n=1594), diff=-0.29 months, p=0.009. Likely false positive: identical analyses in every other subgroup show null pembrolizumab effects.
